status=open
reason=Bash tool is unusable in this execution environment. Every invocation (including with dangerouslyDisableSandbox=true, and identically inside a freshly spawned subagent) fails before running any command with `ENOSPC: no space left on device, mkdir '/tmp/claude-501/-Users-erik-Projects--ddx-exec-wt--execute-bead-wt-ddx-1b3b813c-20260708T210728-f042801c'`. This is a host-level /tmp disk-full condition, not something fixable from within this worktree — there is no shell access at all, so `git`, `go test ./internal/bead/...`, and `lefthook run pre-commit` cannot be run to produce or verify evidence for this bead's AC. The Write/Read/Edit tools do work against the real filesystem (confirmed via a probe write), so the repo disk itself has space; only the /tmp mount backing the Bash sandbox is exhausted. Retry once the host's /tmp is cleared (likely stale claude-501 session directories from concurrent worktrees on this box); no code changes were attempted because there is no way to validate them. Investigation performed before concluding this is an infra blocker (not scoped as no-op): - Read cli/internal/bead/import_postgres.go and cli/internal/bead/migrator.go — importJSONLCorpusToAxon, prepareImportedAxonCorpus, and Migrator.MigrateToAxon already appear substantially implemented (reads active+archive JSONL, preserves inline events via bead.Extra["events"], writes via target.WriteAll). Could not confirm via `go build`/`go test` whether this already satisfies the bead's ACs or whether TestImporter_Apply_WritesToPostgres/PreservesArchiveState/WritesInlineEvents exist or pass, because no command execution is available.
suggested_action=retry with a smart agent

no_changes
